title: saponaria biovulata
intro: Saponaria biovulata, also known as Soapwort, is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white, pink, or purple flowers and edible roots. The plant's roots and leaves contain saponins, which have been used in traditional medicine for their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Saponaria biovulata include soothing skin irritations, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and aiding in digestion. Herbal preparations of Saponaria biovulata include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, often used to treat skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
